description:
Advances: mutagen.coverage, mutagen.mutation_pipeline

Deliverable:
1. Extend `MutagenEx.AstCache.load/2` to accept categorised input via an `opts[:categories]` map (e.g. `categories: %{scope: scope_files, test: test_files}`). The first positional arg stays a flat `[String.t()]` list of all files to load. Categorisation is input-only metadata for diagnostics — NOT stored in the entry shape.
2. The cache entry stays a 2-tuple `{Macro.t(), String.t()}` per mutagen.decision.ast_cache_facade_preserved. NO 3-tuple, NO category tag.
3. The `Pipeline.AstCacheFacade` `@callback load/2` signature is PRESERVED VERBATIM. Existing test fakes must continue to compile and work unchanged.
4. `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex` `phase_ast_cache` now passes both scope files AND test files. The test set is `test_filter.files` (the cited test files, not the full test tree). Phase order stays unchanged.
5. `Baseline.detect_async_modules/1` (baseline.ex:203-210) reads via `AstCache.get(cache, file)` and consumes the returned `{ast, _source}` directly. On `:error` (cache miss), fall back to the pre-`.25` `File.read/1` path as a safety net — log the miss for diagnostics.
6. Revise `.spec/specs/coverage.spec.md` to document the test-file extension in the AstCache contract (likely a new r-number or a revision to r6).
7. `Baseline` tests gain a case where the cache is populated and `File.read` should NOT be called.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: `mix test` green; existing `test/mutagen_ex/ast_cache_test.exs` still passes; new tests cover the categorised-input path and the fall-back
- Behaviors to demonstrate: Baseline can run without re-reading test files from disk when the cache is hot; cache-miss fall-back works; categorised input produces same entry shape as flat input

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/mutagen_ex/test_selector.ex (F19 descoped; do not touch)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/** (S3 owns)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex (S4/S5 own)
- lib/mutagen_ex/ast.ex (S1 owns)
- lib/mutagen_ex/beam_cache.ex (S5 owns)
Allowed touches:
- lib/mutagen_ex/ast_cache.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/pipeline/ast_cache_facade.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex (phase_ast_cache only)
- lib/mutagen_ex/baseline.ex
- test/mutagen_ex/ast_cache_test.exs
- test/mutagen_ex/baseline_test.exs
- .spec/specs/coverage.spec.md
- CHANGELOG.md

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do NOT change the `@callback load/2` signature in `pipeline/ast_cache_facade.ex` — per mutagen.decision.ast_cache_facade_preserved this is a hard rule. Verification will grep the callback.
- Do NOT introduce a `files_by_category/2` function. Consumers look up by file path.
- Do NOT add `:persistent_term`-backed caching anywhere — F19 is descoped per mutagen.decision.f19_descoped.
- Do NOT reorder `phase_tests` and `phase_ast_cache` (that ripple effect was explicitly excluded).
- Cache entry shape must stay `{Macro.t(), String.t()}`. If you introduce a category field, the auditor will reject.

Merge gate:
- [ ] `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` green
- [ ] `mix test` green
- [ ] Determinism test green
- [ ] Pipeline.AstCacheFacade callback signature unchanged (grep verification)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Commit: `mutagen-wrd.25.02: AstCache categorised load + F18 Baseline cache consumer`

IMPLEMENTED
Session: 1932fee7-a5d3-4675-b46d-9a3eca264d33
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/1932fee7-a5d3-4675-b46d-9a3eca264d33.jsonl

Commit: 89698cd
Stage branch: mutagen-wrd.25.3/ast-cache-load (based on epic HEAD b343109)
Stage worktree: /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-ast-cache-load

Files touched (all within Allowed touches):
- lib/mutagen_ex/ast_cache.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/pipeline/ast_cache_facade.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex (phase_ast_cache function body + its one-line call site in do_pipeline's `with` chain)
- lib/mutagen_ex/baseline.ex
- test/mutagen_ex/ast_cache_test.exs
- test/mutagen_ex/baseline_test.exs
- .spec/specs/coverage.spec.md
- CHANGELOG.md

Tests added (8 new):
- MutagenEx.AstCacheTest (describe "load/2 — r9: categorised input"):
  - "s9a: categorised load produces a cache byte-identical to the flat load"
  - "entry shape stays {ast, source} regardless of category presence (r9)"
  - "reader is still called exactly once per file when :categories is set"
  - "load/2 ignores a malformed :categories value (does not crash)"
- MutagenEx.BaselineTest (describe "r9: ast_cache integration (post-`.25.3`)"):
  - "s9b: cache hit — async warning surfaces without reading the file from disk"
  - "s9b: cache hit with async: false — no warning, and still no disk read"
  - "s9c: cache miss — falls back to File.read/1 + parse"
  - "no ast_cache field at all — fallback path stays intact (pre-`.25` behaviour)"

Verification stubs flipped: mutagen.coverage.v6 (new) added with `execute: true`, covering r9. Existing v3 (covers r6) still passing — the AstCache changes are backwards-compatible.

Hard-rule checks:
- `Pipeline.AstCacheFacade.@callback load/2` signature is byte-identical (grep-verified). No positional change, no return-type change.
- Cache entry shape is `{Macro.t(), String.t()}` 2-tuple. Test asserts `{_ast, source} = entry` matching.
- No `:persistent_term`-backed caching introduced (the only references are pre-existing in mutation_runner telemetry counters).
- No `files_by_category/2` consumer API exists (moduledoc text mentions the name only to say it does not exist).
- Phase order unchanged: phase_tests (line 420) -> phase_ast_cache (line 422) in do_pipeline.
- lib/mutagen_ex/test_selector.ex untouched (F19 descoped).

Project verification (Phase 1 gate):
- `mix compile --warnings-as-errors`: green
- `mix test --seed 0`: 561 tests, 0 failures (553 previous + 8 new)
- `mix test` (5x, random seed): all green, no flakes attributable to this change

Deferred must requirements:
- Production wiring of `ast_cache: ast_cache` from `phase_ast_cache` through to `Baseline.run/1`'s input map (i.e., editing `phase_baseline` to pass the cache). Strict reading of this ticket's Allowed touches restricts `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex` to `phase_ast_cache only`; `phase_baseline` is out-of-scope here. Baseline is FULLY cache-aware module-side (deliverable 5 is met for the module contract), and the new s9b/s9c tests verify both the cache-hit and cache-miss paths by passing `:ast_cache` directly in the input. Until the production wiring lands in a follow-up, the live `mix mutagen` pipeline keeps Baseline's pre-`.25` fallback path (`File.read/1` for each cited test file). I tried to `bw create` a follow-up ticket for this single-line plumbing change but it was blocked by the auto-mode classifier as scope creep — the orchestrator can decide whether to file one or fold the wiring into the next stage.

Summary: Extended `AstCache.load/2` with an optional input-only `:categories` map for diagnostic visibility; kept the cache entry shape and the `Pipeline.AstCacheFacade` `@callback load/2` signature byte-identical per `mutagen.decision.ast_cache_facade_preserved`. Mix task's `phase_ast_cache` now loads scope files + cited test files in one pass with `:categories` set. `Baseline.detect_async_modules/1` consumes the cache via `AstCache.get/2` when present and falls back to `File.read/1` + parse on miss (logged). Spec gains `r9` + scenarios s9a/s9b/s9c + verification v6.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: ae38cb61079b7ea9c
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/1932fee7-a5d3-4675-b46d-9a3eca264d33/subagents/agent-ae38cb61079b7ea9c.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 8
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0 (the one-line edit in `do_pipeline`'s `with` chain at lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:422 is the unavoidable call-site change required by the `phase_ast_cache` signature update; mechanically still inside the same file and the only such change; treated as in scope)
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 (test_selector.ex, mutators/**, mutation_runner.ex, ast.ex, beam_cache.ex untouched — verified)

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 6, passed: 6
  - v1 (coverage_runner_test.exs): 12 tests, 0 failures
  - v2 (coverage_runner_test.exs --only scope_filter): 2 tests, 0 failures
  - v3 (ast_cache_test.exs): 14 tests, 0 failures
  - v4 (--only spike c1_test.exs): 1 test, 0 failures
  - v5 (supervision_test.exs): 5 tests, 0 failures
  - v6 (ast_cache_test.exs + baseline_test.exs — NEW, covers r9): 26 tests, 0 failures
- Requirements coverage (one line per `must` requirement actually touched/added by this ticket):
  - mutagen.coverage.r6 (revised to mention test files in flat list): covered by test/mutagen_ex/ast_cache_test.exs (existing assertions on entry shape + once-per-file reads still green). Would fail if entry shape changed away from {ast, source} or if the reader were invoked more than once per file.
  - mutagen.coverage.r9 (s9a: input-only categories metadata, byte-identical cache): covered by test/mutagen_ex/ast_cache_test.exs:164 ("s9a: categorised load produces a cache byte-identical to the flat load") + :180 (entry-shape match `{_ast, source}`) + :196 (reader counter == files length). Would fail if `:categories` ever leaked into entry shape (3-tuple match would explode), if `with_cat != without_cat`, or if categorisation triggered a double-read.
  - mutagen.coverage.r9 (s9b: Baseline consumes cached AST without disk read — module-level capability): covered by test/mutagen_ex/baseline_test.exs:208 ("s9b: cache hit — async warning surfaces without reading the file from disk") using a virtual path that does not exist on disk; the test refute-asserts File.exists?(virtual_file), so any fallthrough to File.read returns :enoent and the async warning disappears. Also :240 (s9b serial cache hit produces no warning, still no disk read).
  - mutagen.coverage.r9 (s9c: cache miss falls back to File.read + parse, logged): covered by test/mutagen_ex/baseline_test.exs:259 ("s9c: cache miss — falls back to File.read/1 + parse") + :308 (no `:ast_cache` field — pre-`.25` fallback path preserved). Would fail if cache miss did NOT fall back (zero warnings against on-disk file) or if the absence of `:ast_cache` broke the legacy code path.
- AstCacheFacade @callback load/2 signature: byte-identical to pre-`.25` (re-read lib/mutagen_ex/pipeline/ast_cache_facade.ex:27-29 — `load(files :: [String.t()], opts :: keyword()) :: {:ok, map()} | {:error, atom(), map()}`). Decision `ast_cache_facade_preserved` honored.
- Intent-level scope concerns:
  - No `files_by_category/2` consumer API introduced (verified via diff scan).
  - No `:persistent_term`-backed caching introduced (only pre-existing references in mutation_runner telemetry).
  - phase_tests → phase_ast_cache order preserved (lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:421-422).
  - Cache entry shape stays 2-tuple `{Macro.t(), String.t()}` (verified by tests via `{_ast, source} = entry` matches).
- Project verification: PASS — `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` exit 0; `mix test` 561 tests, 0 failures.
- Audit-expanded scope (per step 11), if any: none — no prior `AUDIT: BOUNCE → implementer` comment on this ticket.

Verdict: PASS

Source-claim trace (r9 / s9 — capability vs runtime, per audit step 10):
- The deliverable's Allowed touches explicitly restricts `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex` to `phase_ast_cache only`. `phase_baseline` is NOT in Allowed touches.
- r9 statement and decision `ast_cache_facade_preserved` describe consumer behaviour ("Baseline.detect_async_modules/1 ... reads via AstCache.get(cache, file) ... On :error ... falls back"). s9b's `given` clause is conditional on the cache being populated for Baseline — it specifies what Baseline-the-module does under that precondition.
- Neither r9 nor s9b mandates that the production `phase_baseline` step pass `cfg.ast_cache` into `Baseline.run/1`. The deliverable's items 4 (phase_ast_cache loads scope+test files) and 5 (Baseline reads via AstCache.get) are both met module-side.
- Conclusion: r9 / s9 set a capability contract, satisfied here. The production wiring (`phase_baseline` passing the cache through to `Baseline.run/1`) is correctly out of scope for this ticket — the implementer's flagged "deferred must" is a follow-up item, NOT a deliverable self-contradiction or a Gap against this ticket's contract.

Notes:
- Implementer self-flagged a deferred-must: `phase_baseline` does NOT pass `cfg.ast_cache` into `Baseline.run/1`, so the live `mix mutagen` pipeline still uses the disk-read fallback for cited test files. Confirmed by reading lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:695-735 (`input = %{seed: seed, test_filter: test_filter}` — no `ast_cache` key). This does NOT change the verdict because (a) the Allowed-touches glob deliberately scopes `phase_baseline` out, (b) the spec language sets a capability contract not a runtime-wiring claim, and (c) the safety-net fallback is exactly what s9c / the "no ast_cache field" test exercise. A `.25-fu`/`.25.4` follow-up ticket is appropriate to land the production wiring (single-line change: `input = %{... ast_cache: ast_cache}` + plumb through `do_pipeline`'s `with` chain).
- The one-line edit to `do_pipeline`'s `with` chain (mutagen.ex:422) is technically outside the literal "phase_ast_cache only" Allowed-touches glob, but is the unavoidable mechanical call-site update for the new 4-arity signature; not flagged as a violation.
- All hard rules from "Out of Scope (intent)" verified clean: facade callback preserved, no `files_by_category/2`, no `:persistent_term`, phase order intact, entry shape 2-tuple.
- v6 verification stub correctly added with `execute: true` and covers r9. v5 (covers r1+r8) already had execute: true from .25.1/.25.4 supervision work.
- New `Logger.debug` call in `AstCache.log_categories/2` is defensive (`rescue _ -> :ok`); malformed `:categories` value tested (`load/2 ignores a malformed :categories value (does not crash)` in ast_cache_test.exs).

AUDIT: APPROVE
Session: adc4689afabdd3b71
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/1932fee7-a5d3-4675-b46d-9a3eca264d33/subagents/agent-adc4689afabdd3b71.jsonl

Reasoning: Re-verified mechanically and semantically. (1) 8 files touched (matches verifier's count), all within Allowed globs; the only `mutagen.ex` change outside `phase_ast_cache` is the one-line caller update at :422 which is the unavoidable mechanical consequence of the new 4-arity signature — correctly treated in-scope. (2) Hard rules verified clean by direct diff/grep: `@callback load/2` byte-identical (lib/mutagen_ex/pipeline/ast_cache_facade.ex:27-29), no `files_by_category/2` impl (only doc strings stating it does not exist), no new `:persistent_term` (all refs pre-existing in mutation_runner / supervision_test), phase order intact (phase_tests :420 → phase_ast_cache :422), entry shape stays 2-tuple `{ast, source}` asserted by ast_cache_test.exs:180 and :184. (3) Falsifiability of the load-bearing r9 evidence holds: s9b uses a virtual_file that `refute File.exists?` — if cache consumption were broken the fallback would hit `:enoent` and the test's warning assertion would fail (not a happy-path-only test). s9c populates cache with a foreign key and writes the cited file to disk so only the fallback path can produce the asserted warning. s9a asserts byte-identity `with_cat == without_cat` plus a 2-tuple match against the entry — both would fail if `:categories` ever leaked into the entry. `mix test` confirmed: 561 tests, 0 failures.

Deferred-must read: The verifier's classification is correct. r9's literal text describes (a) the `phase_ast_cache` flat-list composition (done — verified by reading lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:584-597 against `test_filter.files`) and (b) downstream consumer behaviour ("Baseline.detect_async_modules/1 ... looks files up by path ... on `:error` falls back") — a module-level capability contract, not a runtime-wiring claim that `phase_baseline` MUST pass `cfg.ast_cache` into `Baseline.run/1`. The Allowed-touches glob explicitly restricts mutagen.ex to `phase_ast_cache only`, so editing `phase_baseline` would itself be a scope violation. Confirmed `phase_baseline` at lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:698 builds `input = %{seed: seed, test_filter: test_filter}` with no `ast_cache` key — production runs the safety-net fallback, which the "no ast_cache field at all" test at baseline_test.exs:308 directly exercises. Production wiring is correctly a follow-up ticket, not a Gap against this contract.

No Out-of-Scope (intent) violations found in the diff. Approving.
MERGE_CONFLICT: smoke red post-rebase (MutagenEx.AstTest find_module_body/2 r8 invariant test failure: before_count=29657 != after_count=29659 atom-count increase)

Orchestrator update: the rebase-worker's MERGE_CONFLICT was a false alarm. The post-rebase `make smoke` used `--max-failures 1` and happened to pick a test seed where a PRE-EXISTING flaky test (`test/mutagen_ex/ast_test.exs:135` "find_module_body/2 does NOT call String.to_atom on the target string (r8 invariant)") landed in a position where the atom-count delta read non-zero. Reproduced the same failure on the bare epic tip in isolation (24605 → 24671 atom growth without any .25.3 code in play), confirming the test is order-dependent, not regression-induced.

5 random-seed smoke runs (0, 1, 42, 100, 12345) on the rebased branch all green: 608/608. Re-attempting ff-merge.

Follow-up ticket to be filed for the flaky-test root-cause fix (originates in .25.2 ast_test.exs, not .25.3).

title: S2: AstCache categorised load + F18 Baseline cache consumer
